artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2024-07-09
artifacts.excerpt:
one of my biggest whitepill moments in coding was early in junior yr of college around this fact

the leetcode problem was “given a positive integer n, return the sum of the first n odd integers” https://t.co/Jqqyfsl9W7

(you prob see the pattern already)

artifacts.title: Lol receipt from 2024-07-09
artifacts.excerpt:
we went back to look through the answers, everyone else had done an O(n) for-loop implementation

“wtf did you use DP? how was yours so much faster?”

they looked at my soln, paused, thought through for a sec, and let out an “…ohhh”

i had literally zero clue what DP meant LOL

artifacts.title: Way receipt from 2024-07-09
artifacts.excerpt:
the pattern was pretty obvious to me — it should always return n squared! i wrote it down, submitted, and my O(1) solution took less time/space than like 99.9% of runs

it was so obvious i didn’t even give it a second thought, i figured everyone did it the same way and moved on

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2024-07-09
artifacts.excerpt:
im sharing this not to brag but to say that outside perspectives on coding tends to be very “oh i could never do that” but at the end of the day coding is just a means to the end of problem solving

have deeper faith in your abilities, don’t count yourself out, it’s all doable
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, right.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2024-11-06
artifacts.excerpt:
like genuinely cooked

once there’s a critical mass of men and women who just don’t care enough to hear the other side bc of the hurt they’ve endured, any shared understanding erodes. rebuilding trust is an order of magnitude more difficult than breaking it down https://t.co/OWCCBex1Xi https://t.co/iBcV36fIzE
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: don, know, want, right.

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2023-12-19
artifacts.excerpt:
imo a major difference between experts and novices is the ability for the former to “zoom in and out”, and more importantly to know which zoom setting a particular problem requires

novices otoh don’t recognize there is more room to zoom in/out than what they’re familiar with
